end_effects.description: Catastrophic physical failure resulting in fire, explosion, or high-energy release.
Exposure of the user or environment to hazardous voltage, toxic materials, or extreme temperatures.
Unintended activation or inability to stop hazardous moving parts.
Violation of statutory environmental or emissions regulations.
Failure to meet mandatory electromagnetic interference (EMI) or regional electrical safety certifications.
Breach of mandatory data privacy, cybersecurity, or communication standards.
Complete inability to power on, initialize, or accept user inputs.
Abrupt and unrecoverable cessation of the primary function during operation.
Total inability to interface, physically or digitally, with required external systems or accessories.
Primary function operates, but at a noticeably degraded speed, range, or capacity.
Product output is erratic, unstable, or of poor quality compared to specifications.
Excessive consumption of power, fuel, or consumable resources during normal operation.
Nuisance user experience due to non-hazardous heat, vibration, or elevated noise levels.
Visible cosmetic damage, wear, or material discoloration that does not affect operation.
Minor sensory anomalies (e.g., harmless clicking, squeaking, or slight odor) with zero functional impact.
